Antwortvariable.first_evidence_snippet:
and species
composition in the Hainich forest in central Germany. The clusters contained all possible combinations of five
broadleaved tree species in one-, two- and three-species clusters (three diversity levels), replicated fourfold (20
one-species, 40 two-species and 40 three-species clusters). We estimated soil water uptake during a summer
dry period

Hauptgruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: Observed tree species on these clusters were Acer pseudoplatanus (sycamore maple), Carpinus betulus (hornbeam), Fagus sylvatica (European beech), Fraxinus excelsior (ash) and Tilia sp. (lime).

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: The study was conducted in the deciduous Hainich forest in central Germany close to the village of Weberstedt.

Trend.evidence_anchor: We could not detect any effects of species identity or diversity on cluster water use.

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: In den Jahren 1978 bis 1982 wurde im Staatswald Burgholz (Solingen) die Arthropodenfauna eines sauren Rotbuchen- und eines Fichtenhochwaldes mit Boden- und Baum-Photoeklektoren untersucht

Trend.evidence_anchor: Die außerordentlich hohen Abundanzen der Sciaridenimagines 1978179 (Tab. 1) erklären sich aus der starken Gradation der Art Ctenosciara hyalipennis (MEIGEN) in beiden Biotopen (DORN 1982). Sie liegen erheblich über den Werten vergleichbarer Untersuchungen im Solling (ALTMÜLLER 1976 und 1979, THIEDE 1977) und Nordschwarzwald (VOLZ 1983). Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: In the mid-1990s, a Spruce Bark Beetle ( Ips typographus L.) outbreak affected the Norway spruce ( Picea abies (L.) Karst.) forests in the higher elevations of the Bavarian Forest National Park in Germany.

Trend.evidence_anchor: The prevailing change is an increase in the cover of already present species such as the dominating Avenella flexuosa, Calamagrostis villosa, or Luzula sylvatica, as well as rare or accompanying species such as Homogyne alpina or Maianthemum bifolium. Only a few species emerged newly on the plots, mainly saplings of pioneer trees such as Betula sp. or light-demanding species such as Rubus idaeus, but a few individuals of these species also were present at the beginning in the area between the plots.

Trend.evidence_anchor: Fagus trees in a neighbourhood with more than 30 % of the canopy’s ‘influence sphere’ occupied by allospecific trees had a higher mean stem increment, a lower increment sensitivity to environmental fluctuation and a smaller growth depression after the 1976 drought than beech trees surrounded by conspecific trees.
Trend.first_evidence_snippet:
the growth response to, and recovery from, selected
stress events. Fagus trees in a neighbourhood with more than
30 % of the canopy’s ‘influence sphere’ occupied by allospecific trees had a higher mean stem increment, a lower
increment sensitivity to environmental fluctuation and a
smaller growth depression after the 1976 drought than beech

map quadrants using both published and unpublished data. Altogether 39 quadrant recordings exist from
the period since 1980.

These were lichens (lichenophyta), mosses (bryophyta), vascular plants (pteridophyta et spermatophyta), birds (aves), butterflies (lepidoptera), carabid beetles (carabidae) and snails (gastropoda).

Antwortvariable.evidence_anchor: Population densities in the native range are usually around 10 – 12 raccoons per 100 ha (Kaufmann 1982) and can reach 333 individuals per 100 ha in urban sites (Riley 1998). Population densities in the non-native range are lower than this, with the highest densities in swamp areas of Northeastern Germany (Müritz National Park) with 6 – 8 individuals per 100 ha (Muschik et al. 2011) and in the urban areas of Bad Karlshafen and Kassel in Central Germany where densities exceed 100 individuals per 100 ha (Hohmann and Bartussek 2011).

Hauptgruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: Raccoons were introduced in different European countries by deliberate or accidental releases occurring since the early twentieth century (Beltrán-Beck et al. 2012). They have become widely established, and are considered a pest in several places due to the economic damage they cause, their threat to public health and negative interactions (competition and predation) with native species (Ikeda et al. 2004; Beltrán-Beck et al. 2012; Vos et al. 2012, 2013). Additionally, they were identified as one of the top ten invasive alien species with the greatest potential to threaten biodiversity in Great Britain (Roy et al. 2014b).

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: The forested Solling mountains probably provide the most comparable habitat to that typically occupied in the native range, and population densities here are 1 – 4 individuals per 100 ha (Hohmann 1998). These lower population densities to comparable habitat in the native range indicate the potential for future population growth in Germany.

Trend.evidence_anchor: The increase from about 9,000 harvested raccoons in 2000/01 to about 71,000 in 2011/12 reflects the extensive amount of suitable habitat for this omnivorous species in Central Europe.

Untergruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: Raccoons were introduced in different European countries by deliberate or accidental releases occurring since the early twentieth century (Beltrán-Beck et al. 2012). Additionally, they were identified as one of the top ten invasive alien species with the greatest potential to threaten biodiversity in Great Britain (Roy et al. 2014b).

Trend.evidence_anchor: The results indicate that open landscape species are particularly threatened. With exception of Lumbricidae (only two threatened species in Germany), species of open landscapes are overrepresented in the group of threatened species in all species groups (Fig. 2). Across all 8 higher taxa, the largest fraction of threatened species is found among species only occurring in open landscapes (O; about 50%).

Trend.evidence_anchor: The results indicate that open landscape species are particularly threatened. With exception of Lumbricidae (only two threatened species in Germany), species of open landscapes are overrepresented in the group of threatened species in all species groups (Fig. 2). Across all 8 higher taxa, the largest fraction of threatened species is found among species only occurring in open landscapes (O; about 50%).

93,000 people live (Stadt Hanau, 2015) (Fig. 2). The Bulau covers
an area of 604 ha and is part of the floodplain of the river Kinzig. It
is a protected area according to the Flora-Fauna-Habitat Directive

Trend.evidence_anchor: The forest community in the Bulau is strongly influenced by groundwater and is referred to as chickweed-oak-hornbeam forest (Stellario-Carpinetum) (Buttler et al., 2003).

founded in 1997). European beech is the dominant tree species in
large areas

respect to water use and their impact on water flows.

functional and phylogenetic diversity

Arten. Ein Literaturvergleich zeigt teilweise sehr ähnliche Ergebnisse (Tab. 5).
